Description: This is a family of silicon planar power Zener diodes designed for voltage stabilization and clipping circuits in high-power applications. They operate in the reverse breakdown (Zener) region to maintain a nearly constant voltage across their terminals despite variations in current or supply voltage, and can also conduct in the forward direction like a standard diode. The series offers a wide range of nominal Zener voltages from 3.0V to 100V, packaged in a DO-41 glass case.

KeyParameters.**Zener Voltage Range (Vz)**: Specific tolerance range per part number (e.g., 1N4727A: 2.85V - 3.15V)
KeyParameters.**Maximum Power Dissipation (Ptot)**: 1.0 W (at Ta=25°C with leads at ambient 8mm from case)
KeyParameters.**Maximum Zener Impedance (Zzt)**: Ranges from 10 Ω (e.g., 3.0V) to 900 Ω (e.g., 100V) at the test current Izt
KeyParameters.**Maximum Regulator Current (Izm)**: Ranges from 275 mA (3.0V) to 12.5 mA (100V) at Ta=25°C
KeyParameters.**Maximum Surge Current (Izm (surge))**: Ranges from 1375 mA (3.0V) to 62 mA (100V) at Ta=25°C
KeyParameters.**Maximum Reverse Leakage Current (IR)**: Ranges from 150 μA (3.0V) to 10 μA (100V) at specified test voltage VR

Functions.FunctionDescription: Negative terminal; voltage is regulated relative to this pin when reverse-biased (Zener operation)
